description: parseFlags (src/bin/cli.js) splits every LIST flag value on a comma (LIST_VALUE_SEPARATOR in write-policy.js) and a repeated LIST flag is last-wins. Acceptance criteria are prose sentences full of commas; one criterion silently becomes N items and the record ships wrong with no error. Fix: a LIST flag contributes exactly one verbatim item per occurrence; repeats append. Drop comma-splitting and retire LIST_VALUE_SEPARATOR; correct the write-policy comment. Affects --scope --acceptance-criteria --depends-on --related-tickets.
title: LIST flags append per occurrence and stop comma-splitting prose
root_cause: LIST type splits on a comma that legitimately appears inside prose list items; repeat overwrites instead of appending so the obvious workaround also fails silently.
fix: In parseFlags: for FLAG_TYPE.LIST push the whole trimmed value as one element and append on repeat (initialize the array on first sight). Remove the split on LIST_VALUE_SEPARATOR and the constant; update the FLAG_TYPE/write-policy comments to say 'repeatable; one item per occurrence'.
acceptance_criteria: Two repeated --acceptance-criteria flags whose values each contain internal commas produce exactly two array items each held verbatim
A single --scope value containing a comma is stored as one item and is not split
LIST_VALUE_SEPARATOR is removed and no code path splits a LIST value
A parseFlags unit test covers repeat-append and the no-split behavior
